rag_rules: Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic in AGREE contracts.
Place component type assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes, not implementation scopes.
Generate AGREE contracts within annex agree {** ... **}; blocks using valid AGREE logical operators and syntax.
generic_rules: Relate visible outputs to visible inputs in type-level guarantees; avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths.
Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations in the same annex for AGREE expressions.
Ensure type consistency between helper declarations and their expressions; use int, real, bool for scalar types.
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicit typed ports or constants are available.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts use assume/guarantee syntax with labeled statements and logical operators like and, or, not(...), and =>.
Constants and equations are declared with eq name : type = expression; and const name : type = value;.
Assignments are used for implementation-level bindings in component implementation scopes.
anti_patterns: Do not reference implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts.
Do not attempt to resolve AADL property names as AGREE variables without explicit model exposure.
Do not invent new identifiers or declarations when AGREE reports unresolved references.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing properties not automatically in AGREE scope
Model dependency staging issues unrelated to contract generation
Validator diagnostics for architecture grounding or fusion
